entities: Arby's Restaurant Group, Inc. and Subsidiaries

note_1_organization.spv_purpose: Serve as franchisor of Arby's restaurants in US, Canada, Turkey, Qatar
note_1_organization.permitted_activities: Limited to owning franchise/development agreements, IP, and entering into securitization-related documents
note_1_organization.license_to_arg: 99-year non-exclusive license to use Securitization IP for company-owned restaurants at 5% royalty on net sales

note_2_accounting_policies.revenue_recognition: Royalties recognized as earned; initial franchise fees deferred and recognized over contractual term after restaurant opening; renewal fees recognized over term after license signed and fee paid
note_2_accounting_policies.intangible_assets: Definite-lived amortized straight-line (20 years for franchise/development agreements); indefinite-lived trademark not amortized, tested annually
note_2_accounting_policies.impairment: Annual test for indefinite-lived intangibles in Q4; no impairment in 2023, 2024, 2025
note_2_accounting_policies.income_taxes: Single-member LLC — not subject to federal/state income taxes; responsibility of ARG
note_2_accounting_policies.fiscal_year: 52 or 53 weeks ending Sunday closest to December 31; 2023, 2024, 2025 all 52 weeks

note_4_revenue_recognition.incentive_program_note: 2025 twelve-month incentive program for strategic initiatives; eligible participants receive monthly payment credits

note_5_securitization.class: 2020 Class A-2 Senior Secured Fixed Rate Notes
note_5_securitization.legal_matters: Various litigation and claims incidental to business; no material adverse effect expected

note_6_related_parties.management_fee_structure: Base $10.4M + variable component based on Retained Collections; 2% annual increases
note_6_related_parties.incentive_program_2025: Twelve-month program with monthly payment credits for franchisees and affiliates

key_observations: Stockholders' deficit of -$267.9M (driven by $380M note receivable from parent)
ARG operating income declined from $221.2M (2023) to $94.9M (2025) — significant compression
Company-owned restaurant sales declined from $1.546B (2023) to $1.382B (2025) — reflecting refranchising and closures
Impairment charges of $9.0M in 2025 (up from $3.2M in 2024)
Management fee to affiliate surged from $66.4M (2024) to $122.8M (2025) — nearly doubled
Long-term debt $859.8M (securitization notes + other); maturity profile shows $8.25M in 2026, $773.4M in 2027
Operating lease assets $435.8M with liabilities $483.5M (current + long-term)
ARG is in compliance with all debt covenants as of December 28, 2025
Debt maturity concentration: $773.4M due in 2027 (anticipated repayment)
